text:
In a quiet village beside gentle hills lived Jenny. She enjoyed reading tales written centuries ago. One starlit night, curious about mythical forest creatures, Jenny ventured deep into woods.

She found tracks. They matched woodland sprites'. She giggled. Then she felt tickled. Suddenly, lights. Something moved. She froze. A tiny fairy greeted her. She was amazed. Fairy explained she dwelled there. Jenny learned fairies helped folks.

From then on, Jenny shared whispers. The village knew. She befriended fairies. They guided her climbs, sang softly under moonlight.

One fairy whispered secrets from far lands. She vowed secrecy.

Years passed. Snow fell. Jenny felt lost. A gust shook leaves. Then steps. Someone whispered.

"Penny-farthester?"

Jenny smiled. She knew. They helped. She shared berries.

That winter, she survived. That spring, fairy gifts. Friends.

Jenny grew strong. Woods taught her.

Mythical creatures were close
